All three tools have returned. The results are comprehensive — and deeply concerning. Here is the complete assessment:

---

```json
{
  "claimSummary": "Maria Kovacs (CUST-4521) filed claim CLM-2025-0042 under policy POL-2024-8847 for a reported rear-end collision at a traffic light, citing damage to the rear bumper and trunk. A certified body shop repair estimate was attached and a police report (#WR-2026-44821) was referenced. The policy is active with collision coverage and a $50,000 limit. However, the claim triggers an exceptionally high number of fraud indicators across all three assessment tools: the damage estimate of $47,800 is 5.2x the vehicle's market value of $9,200; collision coverage was added only 12 days before the incident; the referenced police report cannot be found in the regional registry; the submitting body shop has a history of flagged claims; and the customer has 2 prior fraud investigations, an open investigation, and 4 claims filed this calendar year alone.",

DAMAGE-ESTIMATE-LEVEL: (6) Claimed repair amount ($47,800) is 5.2x the vehicle's market value ($9,200); (7) Reported damage scope (rear bumper, trunk) is inconsistent with a total-loss estimate; (8) Repair estimate submitted by a body shop with prior flagged claims; (9) Estimate confidence rated LOW. CUSTOMER-PROFILE-LEVEL: (10) Customer risk rating: HIGH; (11) Confirmed prior fraud history on account; (12) 2 prior fraud investigations; (13) 1 currently open investigation; (14) 4 claims filed this calendar year; (15) 5 total claims all within the last 3 years; (16) Previous claim of $18,500 (collision) filed only 16 days prior on 2026-05-10; (17) Account payment history flagged as late_payments; (18) Policy and membership only 7 months old (inception 2025-11-02).",
  "fraudSuspected": true,

Key priorities: (1) Independently verify police report #WR-2026-44821 with the issuing authority; (2) Audit the attached body shop repair estimate and the shop's relationship with this customer; (3) Investigate the timing of collision coverage addition relative to the incident date; (4) Review the 3 claims filed on this policy in the past 60 days, including the $18,500 collision claim from 2026-05-10; (5) Cross-reference with the currently open investigation on CUST-4521.",

---

### 🔴 Assessment Highlights

| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| Policy Active | ✅ Yes — Comprehensive, Collision covered, expires 2026-12-31 |
| Coverage Limit / Deductible | $50,000 / $500 |
| Documents Submitted | ✅ Yes — Body shop estimate attached, police report referenced |
| Damage Estimate | $47,800 (vs. vehicle market value of only **$9,200**) |
| Fraud Suspected | 🚨 **YES — 18 compounding fraud indicators** |

### ⚠️ Most Critical Red Flags
1. **Coverage timing:** Collision coverage added **just 12 days before** the reported incident — a classic pre-staging signal.
2. **Unverifiable police report:** Report #WR-2026-44821 **does not exist** in the regional registry.
3. **Grossly inflated estimate:** The claimed $47,800 is **5.2× the car's actual market value** of $9,200, filed as a "total loss" for what was described as bumper and trunk damage.
4. **Flagged body shop:** The repair estimate comes from a shop with a **prior history of fraudulent claims**.
5. **Repeat behavior:** Customer has **2 prior fraud investigations**, **1 currently open**, and filed **4 claims this year** — including a $18,500 collision claim just **16 days ago**.

This claim should be **immediately suspended** and handed to the **Special Investigations Unit (SIU)** before any further action is taken.
variables.judgeReasoning: This claim triggers every major escalation criterion simultaneously, making it one of the highest-risk profiles reviewable. Specific escalation triggers fired: (1) fraudSuspected=true explicitly set in the assessment report; (2) Collision coverage added only 12 days before the incident — a textbook pre-staging indicator and a hard escalation rule; (3) Claimed damage estimate of $47,800 is 5.2x the vehicle's market value of $9,200 — far exceeding vehicle value, another hard escalation rule; (4) Confirmed prior fraud history on the customer account with 2 prior fraud investigations and 1 currently open investigation — hard escalation rule; (5) Police report #WR-2026-44821 is unverifiable — not found in the regional registry, satisfying the unverifiable incident details escalation rule; (6) 4 claims filed this calendar year, 3 claims under this policy within 60 days, and a $18,500 collision claim filed just 16 days prior — satisfying the multiple claims in short period escalation rule. Beyond the hard triggers, compounding risk factors include: the repair estimate was submitted by a body shop with a documented history of flagged claims; the reported damage (rear bumper, trunk) is physically inconsistent with the total-loss categorisation of the estimate; the damage estimate confidence was rated LOW by the estimation engine; the policy itself is only 7 months old; and the customer account carries a high risk rating with a history of late payments. All 6 independent escalation rules are satisfied concurrently. There is no basis whatsoever for APPROVE or MANUAL_REVIEW. This claim must be immediately referred to the Special Investigations Unit (SIU) with a hold on any disbursement.
